---
title: "Year Three"
image: "/assets/mjb_favicon.png"
location: "Dublin City University, Ireland"
---

::: {style="text-align: justify;"}
This page contains a summary of the work I have done during my third year in university. 
:::

## KIMAP Introduction to Management

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fkimap.b.kobe-u.ac.jp%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2Fkimap_ogp_logo.png&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=6c96f821de68b4d050fe1450166e597f87622e1aadf907fc1e3ecbf037bf1a77)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

[Consultancy Report](http://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/projects/KIMAP)

Developed critical understanding of management across for-profit/non-profit sectors, exploring strategic roles (scanning environments, coordination/control), organisational dynamics (differentiation/integration, leadership/diversity/conflict), and paradigms like international/cross-cultural/HR/marketing/supply chain management plus entrepreneurship.

Fostered active collaborative learning through discussions, case studies and strategic mindset development.

Grading: 30% exam, 30% participation, 40% group report.

:::

:::

## Advanced European Economy

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Ffacts.net%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2025%2F05%2F34-facts-about-european-economic-community-formed-1746582511.jpeg&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=f3f89887213edb0465c95238ab7edf864aa09e42b9f6f0699e33eb919d439582)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

[Assignment 1](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yearone), &nbsp; [Assignment 2](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yeartwo)

Developed expertise in EU economic integration, covering the EU Green Deal for sustainability, the EU's global role, policy competition, new Commission dynamics, monetary integration and crisis responses.

Engaged with key materials like Bruegel memos 2024, leadership analyses, and lectures on economic integration.

Grading: 50% assignments, 50% final exam.

:::

:::

## Advanced Econometric Analysis

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftse4.mm.bing.net%2Fth%2Fid%2FOIP.J5L56wG7rar6KiGZ-zH0zQHaE8%3Fpid%3DApi&f=1&ipt=a5aff0311a107a379abd0a02a8eb843901448b45e7a204967a4f20dfc2197d76&ipo=images)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

Developed skills for empirical economic research, starting with stats foundations and linear regression (OLS, inference via t-tests), then advancing to multivariate models, binary outcomes (logit/probit), panel data (fixed effects) and fixes for heteroskedasticity/serial correlation.

Weekly RStudio assignments built hands-on comfort with running regressions, diagnostics, and interpretation.

Grading: 20% assignments, 30% mid-term, 50% final exam.

:::

:::

## Introduction to Chemistry

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsamikshadeore42.github.io%2FEduGamer%2Fimages%2Fchemistry.webp&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=d6c2421e792a5024f898523b7a7d0b25c91f26e93dbf48c51314c1cd1a8e52af)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

Developed foundational chemistry knowledge, progressing from quantum basics (de Broglie wavelength, Bohr model atomic spectra) and molecular geometry/symmetry (VSEPR shapes like trigonal bipyramidal, square planar; rotational axes) to organic chemistry (IUPAC naming alkanes/alkenes, hydrogenation/HBr reactions), and physical chemistry (ideal gas law, partial pressures, kinetic theory speeds, thermodynamics: state functions, expansion work reversible/irreversible). 

Completed weekly assignments applying these concepts with calculations and drawings.

Grading: 90% assignments, 10% exam. 

:::

:::

## International Economy and Finance

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fonline.york.ac.uk%2Fmedia%2Fmageplaza%2Fblog%2Fpost%2Fg%2Fl%2Fglobal-economy.jpg&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=947de6e751e2cab1ff207bf9ccadc83f5bd3dc488404afb5c616e776d059f14f)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

[Mundell-Fleming Model](http://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/projects/international_economy)

Gained core understanding of international finance, spanning financial markets structure, exchange rate determination (fixed/flexible, PPP, money/interest rates), balance of payments (definitions, policy via elasticity/IS-LM/monetarist approaches), international systems (gold standard to Bretton Woods/flexible rates), monetary integration (Euro), offshore markets (Eurocurrency/bonds), derivatives (futures/options/swaps) and crises/policy coordination.
​
Explored open macro policy linkages and future currency system trends through weekly topics.

Grading: 40% in-class contribution, 60% final exam.

:::

:::

## Japanese Legal System

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fpix4free.org%2Fassets%2Flibrary%2F2021-07-08%2Foriginals%2Flegal-system.jpg&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=2fd60ac70efbd5e418bbab84b63fe13a247e5e99630de387aa77c31abff1c275)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

[Assignment 1](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yearone), &nbsp; [Assignment 2](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yeartwo)

Developed foundational knowledge of the Japanese legal system, from general introduction and governmental structure to historical evolution, constitutional rights guarantees, and key areas like intellectual property, criminal/administrative procedure, environmental/tax law.

Explored practical applications through video clips and a special lecture, culminating in a summing-up review.

Grading: 20% attendance, 40% minute papers, 40% final essay. 

:::

:::

## Advertising and Marketing Communications

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fdeclaration.co.uk%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2021%2F04%2FBlog-007-Feature-image.png&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=b08151af7b7a2489e730f5c6179dd87f0b30685f6d591b11c7e8f4db6347491b)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

[Assignment 1](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yearone), &nbsp; [Assignment 2](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yeartwo)

Developed foundational knowledge in advertising and marketing communications, covering strategy/planning, privacy in online environments, global advertising, sustainability/CSR, social well-being impacts, and new technologies in marketing.

Fostered critical analysis by evaluating research from leading journals on current trends and issues.

Grading: 50% group assessment, 50% individual video presentation. 

:::

:::

## Advanced Portfolio Management - Theory and Practice

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.clipartmax.com%2Fpng%2Fmiddle%2F144-1441200_project-portfolio-management-project-management-logo-png.png&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=767ba1f66e4d23325add3c4cea815c9e5eda13efabbce26a0551a5fa3c78d6bb)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

[Assignment 1](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yearone), &nbsp; [Assignment 2](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/studies/yeartwo)

Developed hands-on expertise in advanced portfolio management, blending theory (objectives, asset allocation, mean-variance optimization, efficient frontiers, hedging, behavioral finance) with practical portfolio construction, security selection, risk/return analysis, and real-world cases.

Managed an 8-week live asset investment portfolio, tracking performance impacts from market elements.

Grading: 20% class participation, 10% homework, 30% mid-term exam, 40% final portfolio presentation.

:::

:::

## Intermediate Japanese Language

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.innovativelanguage.com%2Fmembers%2Fjapanesepod101%2Fimages%2Flearning-paths%2Foriginal%2F205_597641a1282de.png&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=ddeddce3764b5c3d8c765cb399f2dc2b52c19fa3072c0ad207c42c2797726053)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

Developed Intermediate Japanese proficiency (JLPT N3 level equivalent) across 6 integrated modules: General Language, Reading, Kanji, Speaking, Writing and Specific Purposes.

Built comprehensive skills through targeted practice in vocabulary, grammar, comprehension, character mastery, oral fluency, composition and practical applications.

Grading: 50% mid-term exams, 50% final exams per module.

:::

:::

## Upper Intermediate Japanese Language

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2}
[![](https://cdn.innovativelanguage.com/members/japanesepod101/images/learning-paths/original/206_599599f43d2a6.png)](https://martinas-jucysbrady.github.io/)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-10}

Developed Upper Intermediate Japanese proficiency (JLPT N2 level equivalent) across 6 integrated modules: General Language, Reading, Kanji, Speaking, Writing and Specific Purposes.

Built comprehensive skills through targeted practice in vocabulary, grammar, comprehension, character mastery, oral fluency, composition and practical applications.

Grading: 50% mid-term exams, 50% final exams per module.

:::

:::
